text.title: Cross-Channel CPA Rollup Worked Example
text.summary: One weekly rollup for a 3-channel SaaS: Meta-paid, Google-search, organic. Shows incrementality + cohort delta.

section.title: Scenario
section.p: A 30-person SaaS spends $4.8k on Meta and $3.2k on Google in 2026-W20. Organic baseline is 60 conversions/week historical. Customer ID coverage is 96%.

section.title: Rollup
section.code:
week=2026-W20
| channel | gross | self_rep | attributed | view_thr | cpa_gross | incr | cpa_incr | d30 | d60 | d90 | | Meta-paid | 4800 | 51 | 32 | 18 | 150 | 22 | 218.18 | .71 | - | - | | Google-search | 3200 | 42 | 28 | 4 | 114 | 21 | 152.38 | .83 | - | - | | Organic | 0 | - | 60 | - | 0 | 60 | 0 | .84 | - | - |
attribution_policy_version: 1.2.0 notes: Meta CPA-incremental ($218) > Meta CPA-gross ($150) — organic baseline absorbs 10 of the attributed conversions. Cohort retention favors Google (+12pp) at higher gross CPA.

section.title: What the rollup tells the team

list.item: Single-touch CPA says Meta = Google. Incremental CPA says Google is 30% cheaper.
D30 retention says Google quality is higher; Meta likely sources lower-quality cohort.
Self-reported Meta (51) vs attributed (32) shows ~37% over-claim from the platform.
Decision: shift 20% of Meta budget to Google for next 4 weeks; re-evaluate.
